 {"id":"bd-2xk1","title":"demo_showcase quality: run fmt/clippy/test gates before landing","description":"# demo_showcase Quality — Run fmt/clippy/test Gates Before Landing

## Purpose

The demo will touch many parts of the codebase. This bead ensures we don’t “ship a broken flagship”.

## Required Gates

Run these locally (and ensure CI passes):

- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`

If demo modifies hot paths or rendering behavior, optionally run the buffer benchmark suite (`cargo bench --bench buffer`) as a sanity check.

## Acceptance Notes

- No formatting drift.
- No clippy warnings.
- All tests pass, including demo compile/smoke tests.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test` all pass after demo_showcase implementation.
- demo_showcase-specific suites are included: compile coverage, headless smoke, JSON snapshots, tour determinism, degradation matrix, and core headless checks.
- Optional PTY E2E tests remain `#[ignore]` but are runnable via scripts and provide actionable artifacts when enabled.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.666703286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:08.399425192Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-10de","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:50.821280848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-1n67","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:51.409960028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2649","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:18.505590317Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:10.570568702Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-31gz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:52.010278354Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.679232573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-3cj0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:20.439486754Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-bqd1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.058244445Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-jfno","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.654855091Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-313","title":"Expand benchmark coverage beyond buffer module","description":"Currently only benches/buffer.rs exists. Add benchmarks for: renderer diff detection, color blending operations, text buffer operations, unicode width calculations.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T20:28:46.184813379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:37:44.657047735Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:37:44.656970329Z","close_reason":"Comprehensive benchmarks added for color blending (creation, blending, conversion, interpolation), text buffer (TextBuffer, EditBuffer, cursor movement, undo/redo), renderer diff detection (identical, single change, row change, scattered, full diff), and unicode width calculations (ASCII, CJK, emoji, grapheme operations). All benchmarks compile and run successfully.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-31gz","title":"demo_showcase docs: README section (how to run + flags + keybinds)","description":"# demo_showcase Docs — README Section (How to Run + Flags + Keybinds)

## Purpose

The flagship demo should be discoverable. Add a README section that explains:

- how to run the demo
- what it demonstrates
- essential keybinds
- tour mode and headless smoke mode
- recommended terminals for best visuals

## Content Requirements

Add a `Demo Showcase` section to `README.md`:

Run:
- cargo run --bin demo_showcase

Tour (unattended):
- cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour --exit-after-tour

Headless smoke:
- cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke

Headless JSON dump (for tests/debugging):
- cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json

Degradation matrix (example manual run):
- cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json --headless-size 50x15 --cap-preset minimal

Document keybinds (or point to the in-app help overlay) and call out terminal recommendations (kitty/alacritty/wezterm/ghostty) for best results.

## Flag Summary (Must Match bd-2iv)

Include a small, explicit list of supported flags (grouped by category):

Interactive:
- --fps <N>
- --tour
- --exit-after-tour
- --max-frames <N>

Headless/testing:
- --headless-smoke
- --headless-dump-json
- --headless-check <name>
- --headless-size <WxH>

Terminal behavior:
- --no-mouse
- --no-alt-screen
- --no-cap-queries

Capability override:
- --cap-preset <name>

Advanced:
- --threaded
- --seed <N>

## Acceptance Notes

- README makes it obvious the demo exists and how to run it.
- Flags documented match the actual CLI (bd-2iv).","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `README.md` contains a clear `Demo Showcase` section with run commands (interactive, tour, headless).
- The documented flag list matches the implemented CLI (`bd-2iv`) including headless size + capability preset controls.
- The README points users to the in-app help overlay for the authoritative keybind list.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.288336317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:14.485670643Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-1gy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:38.026379403Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:36.008108527Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.300717928Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:37.034595590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-35e","title":"demo_showcase UI: toast + notification system (stack + fade)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Toast + Notification System (Stack + Fade)

## Purpose

Toasts make the demo feel like a real application and also give us a constant excuse to show:

- translucent overlays (opacity stack)
- animated alpha fades (time-driven)
- crisp typography + spacing

They also provide immediate feedback when commands/actions occur.

## Behaviors

- Toasts appear in a consistent corner (e.g., bottom-right above the status bar).
- Multiple toasts stack with a small vertical gap.
- Each toast has:
  - severity: Info / Warn / Error
  - title + optional detail line
  - TTL (auto-dismiss) with fade-out
  - optional “action” hint (e.g., `R` retry)

## Rendering Notes

- Background should be semi-transparent with a gradient edge or subtle border.
- Severity affects accent color and icon glyph.
- Clip the toast region with scissor so it never draws into the status bar.

## Integration Points

Toasts should be produced by:

- command palette executions
- tour step transitions
- simulated preview errors/retries
- clipboard/link actions

Implementation suggestion:

- `struct Toast { created_at, ttl, level, title, detail, ... }`
- `struct ToastManager { toasts: VecDeque<Toast> }`
- render in the main render pass after panels but before full-screen overlays.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:23.894150944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:01.203757246Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-1pd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:00.676575823Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-2t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:01.203719095Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:23.906489096Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:00.094359519Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-35e","title":"demo_showcase UI: toast + notification system (stack + fade)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Toast + Notification System (Stack + Fade)

## Purpose

Toasts make the demo feel like a real application and also give us a constant excuse to show:

- translucent overlays (opacity stack)
- animated alpha fades (time-driven)
- crisp typography + spacing

They also provide immediate feedback when commands/actions occur.

## Behaviors

- Toasts appear in a consistent corner (e.g., bottom-right above the status bar).
- Multiple toasts stack with a small vertical gap.
- Each toast has:
  - severity: Info / Warn / Error
  - title + optional detail line
  - TTL (auto-dismiss) with fade-out
  - optional “action” hint (e.g., `R` retry)

## Rendering Notes

- Background should be semi-transparent with a gradient edge or subtle border.
- Severity affects accent color and icon glyph.
- Clip the toast region with scissor so it never draws into the status bar.

## Integration Points

Toasts should be produced by:

- command palette executions
- tour step transitions
- simulated preview errors/retries
- clipboard/link actions

Implementation suggestion:

- `struct Toast { created_at, ttl, level, title, detail, ... }`
- `struct ToastManager { toasts: VecDeque<Toast> }`
- render in the main render pass after panels but before full-screen overlays.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- Toasts render as translucent cards with consistent spacing, typography, and severity styling.
- Toasts stack with a gap and auto-dismiss after TTL with a smooth fade.
- Toast region is clipped so it never draws into the status bar.
- Core actions (palette commands, tour steps, paste, resize, simulated preview error) generate toasts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:23.894150944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:19:20.930723661Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-1pd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:00.676575823Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-2t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:01.203719095Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:23.906489096Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:00.094359519Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35g","title":"demo_showcase runtime: App state machine (modes, focus, event routing)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — App State Machine (Modes, Focus, Routing)

## Purpose

Define a clean “brain” for the demo so the UI remains coherent as features accumulate.

## Core State

Suggested structure:

```rust
enum Mode {
    Normal,
    Help,
    CommandPalette,
    Tour,
}

enum Focus {
    Sidebar,
    Editor,
    Preview,
    Logs,
}

struct App {
    mode: Mode,
    focus: Focus,
    paused: bool, // e.g. focus lost

    // Panel state
    sidebar: SidebarState,
    editor: EditorState,
    preview: PreviewState,
    logs: LogState,

    // Overlay state
    palette: PaletteState,
    help: HelpState,
    tour: TourState,

    // UX polish
    toasts: ToastQueue,
    last_resize: Option<(u32,u32)>,
}
```

## Event Routing Rules

1. **Global shortcuts first** (quit/help/palette/tour/debug/invalidate)
2. **System events next** (resize, focus gained/lost)
3. **Mode-specific** handling (help/palette/tour)
4. **Focused panel** receives remaining key/mouse events

This prevents conflicts and keeps behavior predictable.

## Action Layer (Optional but Recommended)

Instead of mutating state directly in input handlers, parse events into actions:

```rust
enum Action {
    Quit,
    ToggleHelp,
    TogglePalette,
    ToggleTour,
    CycleFocus(i32),
    Editor(EditorAction),
    Sidebar(SidebarAction),
    Preview(PreviewAction),
    Logs(LogsAction),
    Resize(u32,u32),
    FocusChanged(bool),
}
```

This makes tour mode trivial: it just emits Actions.

## Focus / Hit Testing Integration

Mouse clicks should:

- map screen coords → HitGrid id
- translate id → semantic action (e.g. “sidebar item 3”)
- update focus + selection consistently

## Output

A well-defined state machine that later UI beads can build on without “spaghetti input handling”.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Modes are explicit (Normal/Help/Palette/Tour)
- [ ] Focus routing is explicit and consistent
- [ ] Global shortcuts take precedence and never conflict
- [ ] Resize and focus events update app state predictably
- [ ] HitGrid click IDs map cleanly to semantic actions
- [ ] Tour mode can drive the app via the same action pathway","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:36.517144360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:00:33.568945954Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35g","depends_on_id":"bd-1m9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:59.454327871Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35g","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:36.530331471Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35g","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:56.645724337Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35g","depends_on_id":"bd-2mu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:54.177006201Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35g","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:51.610250289Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35rk","title":"demo_showcase features: HitGrid + mouse interactions (clickable UI + hover/pressed)","description":"# demo_showcase Features — HitGrid + Mouse Interactions (Clickable UI + Hover/Pressed)

## Purpose

A “turned up to 11” demo should be *clickable*. OpenTUI’s `HitGrid` is how we map mouse coordinates to UI actions.

This bead ensures mouse interaction feels real and demonstrates:

- hit testing correctness
- hit scissoring correctness (no clicks through clipped regions)
- keyboard/mouse parity (same actions available)

## Hit ID Scheme

Define a stable mapping from hit IDs → actions.

Example approach (demo-only):

- Reserve ID ranges by component:
  - `1000-1999` chrome buttons
  - `2000-2999` sidebar rows
  - `3000-3999` editor area
  - `4000-4999` preview toggles
  - `5000-5999` log rows
  - `9000+` overlay controls

The exact numbers aren’t important; stability and debuggability are.

## Rendering Requirements

- Every interactive element registers hit areas during render:
  - `Renderer::register_hit_area(x, y, w, h, id)`
- Use `Renderer::push_hit_scissor(rect)` around clipped lists so hit areas never extend beyond visible rows.

## Interaction Requirements

- Click to focus panels.
- Click sidebar item to switch section.
- Click log row to select.
- Click overlay buttons (close/next/pause).

Optional polish:

- Hover styling (if mouse move events are available): subtly highlight hovered row.
- Pressed styling: show a brief “pressed” state on click.

## Acceptance Notes

- No hit areas fire outside the visible scissor regions.
- Mouse interactions work even when the UI is resizing.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- A stable hit-id scheme is defined (reserved ranges per component) and used consistently.
- Click interactions work for: top bar buttons, sidebar rows, overlay controls, and selectable log rows.
- Hit scissoring is correct: clipped regions cannot be clicked outside the visible area.
- Visual feedback exists for hover (when available) and pressed states (on click).
- Keyboard/mouse parity holds: every click action has an equivalent keyboard path.
- When mouse is disabled (`--no-mouse` or equivalent gating), the UI remains fully operable via keyboard and does not show misleading click hints.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:27:55.640771011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:16:36.041304145Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-1al","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:27.303690270Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-1e4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:26.192658052Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-1fu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:26.754711998Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-1m9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:25.037470993Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:25.595167417Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-37p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:27:55.656569873Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35rk","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:17.024063982Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-37p","title":"[EPIC] demo_showcase: advanced features prove-out (graphemes, hitgrid, hyperlinks, pixels)","description":"# Epic: demo_showcase — Advanced Feature Prove‑Out

## Purpose

Ensure the demo app explicitly and recognizably exercises OpenTUI’s “wow” features:

- Grapheme pool rendering (multi-codepoint emoji / ZWJ sequences)
- Hyperlinks via OSC 8
- Hit testing grid + mouse interactions
- Pixel-level rendering primitives (supersampling / grayscale)
- Scissor + opacity stacks in nested UI contexts

## Key Principle

These features must not appear as isolated “hello world” snippets.
They should be integrated into believable UI behaviors (preview panels, clickable controls, tooltips, etc.).","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Grapheme pool is exercised with a visible “this would break without pool” string
- [ ] Hyperlink cells are rendered and clickable in supported terminals
- [ ] Mouse click/scroll drives real navigation via `HitGrid`
- [ ] PixelBuffer/GrayscaleBuffer output is integrated into the UI, not a separate screen
- [ ] At least one overlay uses opacity stack + blending for a glass effect","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:34.519122126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:44.123727790Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","epic","features"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37p","depends_on_id":"bd-1co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:34.541866115Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3o0","title":"demo_showcase runtime: tour mode driver (script executor + deterministic timing)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Tour Mode Driver (Script Executor + Deterministic Timing)

## Purpose

Tour mode is how we get:

- unattended demos (screen recordings)
- deterministic behavior for tests/smoke runs
- a guided narrative that proves features in a clear order

This bead implements the runtime that executes the tour script defined in `bd-1gy`.

## Behaviors

- CLI flag --tour starts tour immediately.
- Tour runs a fixed sequence of steps:
  - changes focus/panels
  - triggers overlays (command palette/help)
  - scrolls lists/logs
  - types/pastes into editor
  - triggers preview animations / errors / recovery

Determinism:

- no randomness
- timing driven by a monotonic clock + fixed durations
- any synthetic inputs are generated by the tour driver

Automatic termination (for recordings/E2E):

- If `--exit-after-tour` is enabled (see `bd-3vjm` / `bd-2iv`), the app should exit 0 when the final tour step completes.
- `--max-frames` acts as a hard safety bound; it should never be required for a normal tour, but it prevents hangs.

## Interactive + Headless

The tour driver must work in BOTH:

- interactive mode (real renderer)
- headless mode (for CI regression tests)

Headless mode should be able to execute the tour and emit JSON markers that tests can snapshot (see `bd-bqd1`).

## Implementation Approach

- Introduce a TourRunner in the demo binary:
  - state: TourState { step_idx, step_started_at, paused, ... }
  - fn tick(now, app, out_actions)

- The tour runner should not directly draw; it only manipulates app state and injects actions.

- When the tour wants to show something, it should set a TourHighlight { rect, message } that the tour overlay bead renders.

## Acceptance Notes

- cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour produces a stable “demo story” without user input.
- Tour can be paused/resumed and cleanly exited.
- Tour never leaves the app in a broken focus/input state when it ends.
- With `--exit-after-tour`, the process terminates cleanly at completion.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour` produces a complete, deterministic feature walkthrough without user input.
- Tour driving works in both interactive mode and headless mode (headless emits stable tour step markers in JSON).
- Tour controls work: next/prev, pause/resume, and exit without leaving focus/input in a broken state.
- With `--exit-after-tour`, the process exits 0 when the final step completes and the terminal is restored cleanly.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:18.030533207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:16:55.587961203Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-1gy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:16.254927712Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:18.042919278Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:16.993258401Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-2t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:18.262929423Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:17.664069555Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oqa","title":"demo_showcase runtime: event/log routing (emit_event + set_log_callback -> logs panel)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Event/Log Routing (emit_event + set_log_callback → Logs Panel)

## Purpose

OpenTUI includes an event/log callback system (`src/event.rs`). The demo should use it so we:

- prove the module works
- get a realistic logs stream without sprinkling UI code with ad-hoc logging

## What To Implement

- At demo startup:
  - call `set_event_callback` and `set_log_callback`
  - route events/logs into the demo’s internal `LogModel` (ring buffer)

- Provide a small helper API in demo-only code:
  - `fn log_info(msg)` / `fn log_warn(msg)` / `fn log_error(msg)`
  - optionally `emit_event("action", json)` for structured actions

## Concurrency Notes

Callbacks are `Send + Sync` and stored behind a `Mutex`. Keep callbacks fast:

- push into a lock-free-ish queue (or just a bounded `VecDeque` behind a `Mutex` owned by the app)
- avoid allocating large strings in hot paths

## Acceptance Notes

- Core app actions (palette commands, tour steps, resize, focus changes) generate log entries.
- Logs panel reflects these entries without frame hitching.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- demo_showcase installs OpenTUI event and log callbacks at startup and routes them into the app’s log model.
- Core user actions (palette commands, tour steps, resize/focus changes) emit visible log entries.
- Callback handlers are lightweight enough to avoid frame hitches.
- The logs panel shows these entries without ad-hoc logging scattered across rendering code.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:21.888226798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:38.197559204Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oqa","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:21.908976801Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oqa","depends_on_id":"bd-2c6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:15.671602532Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oqa","depends_on_id":"bd-hc0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:15.124481842Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ot0","title":"demo_showcase runtime: optional ThreadedRenderer backend (--threaded)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Optional ThreadedRenderer Backend (`--threaded`)

## Purpose

OpenTUI has a `ThreadedRenderer` that moves terminal I/O off the main thread. The flagship demo should be able to showcase this as an optional mode.

This is not required for the first “works and looks amazing” milestone, but it’s an impressive proof point for real applications.

## Behavior

- CLI flag: `--threaded`
- When enabled, the demo uses `opentui::renderer::ThreadedRenderer` instead of `Renderer`.
- The UI + state machine should behave the same.

## Design Constraint

`Renderer` and `ThreadedRenderer` have different ownership models (threaded moves buffers/pools through channels). To avoid duplicating the whole app:

- Introduce a small demo-only backend wrapper:
  - `enum Backend { Direct(Renderer), Threaded(ThreadedRenderer) }`
  - helper methods: `buffer()`, `present()`, `resize()`, `set_title()`, `set_cursor()`

## GraphemePool Note (Important)

If tour/unicode panels use grapheme pool content, ensure that the threaded path does not leak grapheme refs:

- Avoid using `ThreadedRenderer::clear()` if it doesn’t clear with pool.
- Prefer explicit `buffer().clear_with_pool(grapheme_pool(), bg)` / `resize_with_pool` patterns.
- If the library needs a small fix (pool-aware clear/resize convenience), file a follow-up bead or patch the library directly.

## Acceptance Notes

- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --threaded` works and exits cleanly.
- Rendering remains smooth and input remains responsive.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:45.107585716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.962836338Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:45.142106801Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:18.844522494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.962802535Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.396568255Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3pe","title":"demo_showcase UI: help overlay (F1) + feature legend","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Help Overlay (F1) + Feature Legend

## Purpose

The help overlay makes the demo self-contained and also proves multiple library features:

- overlay rendering + opacity stack (glass modal)
- scissor clipping (scrollable help content)
- hyperlink rendering via OSC 8 (LinkPool)

It also prevents the “what do I press?” problem for first-time viewers.

## Content Requirements

### Keybinds

Include (at minimum):

- `Ctrl+Q` quit
- `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` focus next/prev panel
- `Ctrl+P` command palette
- `F1` help
- `Ctrl+T` tour start/stop
- editor keys (arrows, word movement, wrap toggle, undo/redo)
- log/preview navigation keys

### Mouse

- click to focus panels
- click to activate buttons
- scroll to scroll lists/logs

### Feature Legend

Make it explicit where each OpenTUI feature is visible in the app:

- RGBA alpha blending (preview overlays / glass modals)
- scissor stack (sidebar/log scroll clipping)
- opacity stack (overlays + tinted UI sections)
- grapheme pool (unicode panel)
- OSC 8 links (logs + help)
- hitgrid (clickable UI)
- pixel buffers (preview panel)
- diff rendering + sync output (perf/inspector + smoothness)

### Clickable Links

Include a small “Docs / Links” section with OSC 8 hyperlinks:

- repo URL
- unicode width reference

The links must be allocated through `renderer.link_pool()` and applied via `Style::with_link`.

## Rendering Notes

- Help content should be scrollable when terminal is small.
- Use clear typography hierarchy: title, section headers, monospace keycaps.
- Use hit areas for the “Close” button and for any tab-like subviews.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:04.642163943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:05.666034752Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-1al","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:04.565909821Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-1ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:05.666002312Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:05.092958035Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:04.656207314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3vjm","title":"demo_showcase runtime: deterministic termination controls (--max-frames / --exit-after-tour)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Deterministic Termination Controls

## Purpose

We need deterministic ways to stop the app without human input so we can:

- run real PTY-based E2E tests without brittle "send keys" logic
- record unattended videos reliably (tour ends -> app exits)
- keep runs bounded for CI and debugging

## Required Controls

### 1) `--max-frames <N>` (Hard Frame Budget)

- After the demo has **presented N frames**, exit 0.
- Count frames consistently across modes (interactive + headless).
- Print a stable summary line on exit (useful for tests/logs), e.g.:
  - `EXIT_OK reason=max_frames frames=<N> last_dirty_cells=<K>`

### 2) `--exit-after-tour` (Tour Completion Exit)

- When tour mode reaches the last step and completes it, exit 0 automatically.
- The exit should be clean:
  - restore cursor
  - leave alt screen (if used)
  - disable mouse (if enabled)
- Tour should still be escapable with user input; this flag only adds an automatic exit at completion.

## Integration Notes

- Flag parsing belongs in `bd-2iv`, but the **run-loop semantics** and **tour end handling** live here.
- Headless mode (`bd-2el`) should also honor these controls so tests can bound runtime.

## Why This Matters

Without deterministic termination, we end up with flaky tests and awkward demo workflows.
These controls make the demo feel "professional" to develop and validate.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `--max-frames <N>` exits 0 after presenting N frames in interactive mode and in headless smoke mode.
- `--exit-after-tour` exits 0 when the final tour step completes (both interactive and headless tour runs).
- Exits are clean: cursor restored, mouse disabled (if enabled), and alt screen exited (if used).
- A stable summary line is printed on exit for deterministic debugging/logging.
- PTY E2E tests can rely on these controls to avoid sending synthetic keypresses.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.678047128Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:26.318650041Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.707235213Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.718517533Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-3o0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.723946582Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.712843797Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-61g0","title":"demo_showcase e2e: PTY harness + ANSI capture (real terminal sequences, ignored by default)","description":"# demo_showcase E2E — PTY Harness + ANSI Capture (Ignored by Default)

## Purpose

Headless tests prove the render pipeline is correct, but they cannot prove real terminal I/O behavior.

This bead adds true end-to-end tests by running the demo under a pseudo-terminal (PTY), capturing ANSI output bytes, and asserting on key invariants:

- alternate screen entry/exit
- cursor hide/show
- mouse enable sequences (if enabled)
- synchronized output enable (if supported)
- OSC 8 hyperlink sequences appear (and do NOT appear when disabled)

## Approach (Unix)

- Use libc openpty / fork+exec (or an equivalent minimal PTY approach) to spawn:
  - cargo run --all-features --bin demo_showcase -- --tour --exit-after-tour --max-frames 600 --fps 30

Why both exit controls?

- `--exit-after-tour` is the intended deterministic terminator.
- `--max-frames` is a hard safety bound so the PTY test cannot hang if tour logic regresses.

- Capture stdout from the PTY master.

## Assertions

Assert the output stream contains, in some order:

- enter alt screen (CSI ? 1049 h) when alt screen enabled
- leave alt screen (CSI ? 1049 l) on exit
- hide cursor (CSI ? 25 l) and show cursor (CSI ? 25 h)
- mouse enable sequences (1000/1002/1006) when mouse enabled
- synchronized output enable (CSI ? 2026 h) when supported/enabled

Hyperlink coverage:

- run one case with default settings and assert at least one OSC 8 sequence appears (ESC ] 8 ; ; ...)
- run one case with `--cap-preset no_hyperlinks` (or equivalent disable) and assert OSC 8 sequences are absent

Optional (high-value): threaded mode parity

- run a second spawn with `--threaded` and assert the same terminal lifecycle sequences appear

Also assert:

- demo exits 0 within a timeout
- output is non-trivial (bytes > some threshold)

## Logging / Artifacts

- Use the structured logger utilities in `tests/common/harness.rs` to:
  - log key milestones (spawned, bytes captured, exit status)
  - write captured ANSI bytes to artifacts (both raw + hex/escaped view)

Controlled via HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1.

## Test Placement

- Mark PTY tests as #[ignore] by default to avoid CI flakiness.
- Provide a script (separate bead) to run them locally and collect artifacts.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- An ignored PTY-based test suite can spawn `demo_showcase` with `--tour --exit-after-tour --max-frames` and capture a non-trivial ANSI output stream.
- Assertions cover: alt screen enter/exit, cursor hide/show, mouse enable (when enabled), and sync output enable (when supported/enabled).
- Hyperlink behavior is covered: OSC 8 sequences present by default and absent under `--cap-preset no_hyperlinks`.
- Optional: a threaded run case (`--threaded`) also passes the same terminal lifecycle assertions.
- On failure, artifacts include raw and escaped/hex output when `HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:04.613268330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:54.313815556Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:27.862668126Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:04.631306455Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-3o0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:28.902157577Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-3vjm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:19.691230196Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:26.694765952Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-8yf","title":"Optimize BufferDiff::compute() with pre-allocated Vec","description":"BufferDiff::compute() creates Vec::new() for changed_cells. Could pre-allocate with estimated capacity based on typical change rate, or use a reusable scratch buffer.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T16:54:52.939084594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:16:53.872340399Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:16:53.871467724Z","close_reason":"Pre-allocated changed_cells Vec based on total size heuristic; cargo check/clippy/fmt clean","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
